question_title: Will at least 30 organisations be listed as signatories of the EU's General-Purpose AI Code of Practice as of July 23, 2026?
summary: The EU GPAI Code of Practice signatory page currently lists 23 distinct organisations (verified directly on the EC contents-code-gpai page and corroborated by the aiprovidertrust tracker). Reaching the 30 threshold by July 23, 2026 requires 7 new signatories in about 10 days — well above the list's slow historical growth. While the August 2, 2026 enforcement deadline creates real pressure to sign, any surge would predominantly land in the final days before Aug 2, after the July 23 cutoff. A backlog release or unusually fast deadline rush gives modest upside, but the modal outcome is that the count remains in the mid-to-high 20s by July 23. Probability ~11%.

factors.description: Authoritative current count is 23 distinct organisations, confirmed by two independent sources. Need +7 in ~10 days to reach 30.
The list has grown very slowly (roughly 20-26 range over nearly a year); a +7 jump in 10 days would be well above any observed trend.

The Aug 2, 2026 enforcement deadline creates late-signing pressure, but any surge would mostly cluster in late July/early August — largely after the July 23 resolution date. Some front-loading possible.
Commission notes some signatories may not appear immediately, allowing a possible backlog batch release before resolution.

reasoning:
## Forecast: 11% (NO likely)

**Question:** Will ≥30 organisations be listed as signatories of the EU GPAI Code of Practice by July 23, 2026?

**Key findings:**
- The authoritative EC page (contents-code-gpai) currently lists **23 distinct organisations**, verified directly and corroborated by an independent tracker (23 as of July 5, 2026).
- Reaching 30 requires **+7 signatories in ~10 days** — well above the list's slow historical growth (roughly 20–26 range over nearly a year).
- The Aug 2, 2026 enforcement deadline creates real signing pressure, but any surge would mostly cluster in the final days before Aug 2 — **after** the July 23 resolution date.
- A backlog release or unusually fast deadline rush provides modest upside, but the modal outcome is the count staying in the mid-to-high 20s.

**Probability: 0.11**
sources_consulted: [Who signed the EU GPAI Code of Practice? · AI Provider Trust Registry](https://aiprovidertrust.com/questions/gpai-code-of-practice/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, as of 23:59 UTC on July 23, 2026, at least 30 distinct organisations are named in the "Signatories of the code of practice" section of the European Commission AI Office's General-Purpose AI Code of Practice page (https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-code-practice). Each organisation listed by name in that section counts once, including any organisation noted as having signed only one chapter (for example, xAI is listed as a Safety and Security chapter signatory and counts as one organisation). Otherwise the question resolves NO.
fine_print: If the signatory list is temporarily unavailable, resolution waits until it is accessible again. If the list has permanently moved to another page, the equivalent official European Commission AI Office signatory listing is used; the signatory section may appear on the linked page itself or on a sub-page reachable from it. Counting is based solely on distinct named organisations displayed in the signatory listing; footnotes clarifying a signatory's chapter-level participation do not add to the count. If the European Commission ceases to publish a signatory list entirely before the resolution date, the question is annulled.

factors.description: Authoritative current count is 23 distinct organisations (EC contents-code-gpai page, confirmed by aiprovidertrust tracker July 5). Need +7 in ~10 days to reach 30.
List has grown very slowly historically (~20-26 range over nearly a year); +7 in 10 days would be well above any observed trend.
Aug 2, 2026 enforcement deadline creates late-signing pressure, but the surge would mostly cluster in late July/early Aug, largely AFTER July 23. Some front-loading possible.
Commission notes some signatories may not appear immediately (possible backlog release), giving small upside.

input.anchor: Current count 23 (EC official page); threshold 30 requires +7 in 10 days, far above the list's slow historical growth rate.
input.assessment: Current clean count is 23 (two independent sources agree). Threshold is 30 by July 23, 2026, needing 7 new signatories in ~10 days. Historical growth of this list has been slow. The Aug 2 enforcement deadline creates pressure, but the bulk of any last-minute wave would arrive in the final days before Aug 2, which is after the July 23 resolution date. A +7 jump in 10 days is well above trend; possible via a backlog release or deadline rush but not the modal outcome.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether a deadline-driven signing surge front-loads before July 23 vs. clusters right before Aug 2; whether there's a backlog of confirmed-but-unlisted signatories.
input.tool_audit: research() sub-agent gave good context but couldn't read the dynamic list precisely. fetch_url on contents-code-gpai successfully returned the rendered signatory list (23 orgs) — the key data point. web_search corroborated with aiprovidertrust (23 as of July 5).
input.process_reflection: Smooth. The decisive step was fetching the correct sub-page (contents-code-gpai) rather than the main ai-code-practice page, which lacks the list. A tool that reliably renders dynamic EC pages would help.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors and the assessment against the trace. No errors that would change the forecast.

FACTOR 1 (count=23, need +7) — CORE CLAIM STRONGLY GROUNDED. Although the nested research sub-agent could not read the live count, the MAIN agent subsequently ran fetch_url on https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/contents-code-gpai (the sub-page the resolution URL redirects/points to) and got an explicit itemized list of exactly 23 distinct organisations (Accexible, AI Studio Delta, Aleph Alpha, ... WRITER, xAI). xAI is correctly counted as one org, exactly matching the resolution criteria's counting method. Independently corroborated by aiprovidertrust (23 as of July 5, 2026). This is the authoritative source named in the resolution criteria — excellent resolution alignment. logit=-1.8 direction correct.

FACTOR 2 (slow growth) — Supported: ~26 in Aug 2025, clustering 23-27 through 2026. Direction correct.

FACTOR 3 (Aug 2 enforcement pressure clusters after July 23) — Well-reasoned; Aug 2, 2026 enforcement date confirmed in trace. Positive logit appropriate.

FACTOR 4 (possible backlog release) — Supported by Commission's noted continuous-update/lag caveat. Small positive, fine.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: Not applicable — nothing claimed to already resolve YES; count (23) is below threshold.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Strong. Agent used the exact authoritative EC listing, applied the specified counting rule (xAI = 1), and evaluated the precise 30-threshold and July 23 timeframe.

WORLDVIEW CONSISTENCY: Related worldview entries concern specific-company sign-ups (Apple/Meta, Anthropic/Mistral), not the count. No numeric sub-forecast on the count exists; no contradiction. Research entry 4ea20464 matches the trace (23 confirmed).

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate matches. Getting +7 net new distinct orgs in 10 days, against a list that added only a handful over ~11 months, is well above trend even accounting for deadline front-loading and backlog. ~11% is well-calibrated; I land in the same 10-13% range. Anchor (23) and probability are consistent — the departure from a coin flip is evidence-driven (a strong, verified -1.8 factor), not narrative-driven.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Genuine and substantive — constructs a coherent alternative (base may be understated at 26-28; deadline-driven front-loading; backlog batch release). Not a token gesture.

SELF-CONFIDENCE: 0.72 is appropriately calibrated given the modest source variance (23 vs 26-27 across vintages).

MINOR NOTE (non-material): The tool_audit narrative could read as ambiguous about which agent read the list, but its factual statement ("fetch_url on contents-code-gpai successfully returned the rendered signatory list, 23 orgs") is accurate for the main agent. Tool metrics show research at 50% error and some fetch_url errors, but the agent does not falsely claim zero failures. No issue rising to warn.
